mechanism_narrative: Elevated federal funds rates increase the discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns are not significantly lower when FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.0%.

mechanism_narrative: The unemployment rate crossing above 4.0 percent signals labor market deterioration and elevated recession probability, triggering risk-off sentiment and negative equity returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in more than 65 percent of instances when UNRATE exceeds 4.0 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month increases in real sales and housing starts confirm synchronized consumer and housing demand expansion, driving positive forward equity returns over the quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive changes in RSAFS and HOUST yield negative 60-day forward returns or produce a Rank IC below 0.02 over rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Higher industrial production index values reflect stronger near-term economic activity and corporate output capacity, generating positive short-term equity returns within one week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns show a negative correlation with INDPRO levels or Rank IC falls below 0.01 over any 1-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: The consumer price index exceeding 305 triggers expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, compressing equity valuations and producing negative forward returns.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day returns dominate when CPIAUCSL remains above 305 during a tightening cycle, or signal accuracy drops below 50 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Rising nonfarm payrolls combined with a declining unemployment rate confirms robust labor market expansion and wage growth, supporting positive equity returns over the month.
failure_condition: Divergent labor signals (PAYEMS up, UNRATE down) yield negative forward returns or interaction term loses statistical significance in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP falling below 22000 billion chained dollars signals economic stagnation and reduced corporate earnings capacity, driving negative forward returns over the quarter.
failure_condition: S&P 500 generates positive 60-day returns in more than 60 percent of quarters when GDPC1 drops below 22000.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production during a monetary tightening cycle indicates supply-side contraction and recession onset, driving negative equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Negative industrial production changes during tightening regimes produce positive forward returns or Rank IC remains below 0.03.
